questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports record management in official duties and responsibilities?
options: Current files with status updates at each control point.
questions.explanation: Record management depends on keeping files current and updating status at each control point so the matter remains reviewable.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on performance standards within Duties & Responsibilities?
options: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Set measurable targets, monitor progress, and correct deviations. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates governance discipline in official duties and responsibilities?
questions.explanation: Governance in official duties and responsibilities is strongest when officers follow the approved procedure and keep the full record needed for review.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain duties & responsibilities compliance in Duties & Responsibilities?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Duties & Responsibilities, which action best demonstrates duties & responsibilities risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective duties & responsibilities, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure administrative ethics
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports grievance handling in official duties and responsibilities?
options: Fair complaint review through timely documented steps.
questions.explanation: Grievance handling remains defensible when complaints are reviewed through fair, timely, and documented steps.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling duties & responsibilities, which choice reflects proper performance management standards?
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in duties & responsibilities Reference: Duties & Responsibilities - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which response best preserves discipline and conduct in official duties and responsibilities?
options: Consistent response to misconduct under approved policy.
questions.explanation: Discipline and conduct are sustained when misconduct is addressed consistently under approved policy rather than through arbitrary exceptions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which office has the ultimate duty for control of votes to the Federal Executive Council?
questions.explanation: Accounting Officers bear the ultimate duty for vote control and must account to the Federal Executive Council for the use of public funds.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Can an officer with delegated expenditure control sub-delegate that accountability without approval?
options: Yes, with the approval of the Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: No. Financial Regulation 404 provides that an officer must not sub-delegate expenditure-control accountability, wholly or partly, without the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote. The question therefore tests the limit on delegated financial authority.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What type of control should exist for the collection of all revenues under the Accounting Officer?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1603(g) emphasizes the need for an adequate system of internal control over the collection of all revenues under the Accounting Officer. The item therefore tests the control structure expected in revenue administration.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which action is forbidden when a non-revenue officer receives public money in the course of duty?
options: Supporting the lodgement with a paying-in form.
questions.explanation: An officer who receives public money in the course of duty must not delay its lodgement, because delay weakens accountability over public funds.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: From what are the duties of Administrative and Professional Officers derived?
options: The needs of the public alone.
Directives issued only by the Permanent Secretary.
questions.explanation: The duties of Administrative and Professional Officers derive from the system of government because their roles are designed to support how government is organised and operated.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: How should deadlines for service delivery be set?
options: By the political party in power.
By the personal preference of the Head of Department.
Realistically, and they should be communicated to users.
questions.explanation: Deadlines for service delivery should be set realistically and communicated to users so that expectations are clear and services can be measured against known standards.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should officers controlling votes ensure for services rendered?
options: Settlement within the same financial year.
Routine deferral of payments to the next financial year.
Transfer of all outstanding claims to suspense accounts.
questions.explanation: Officers controlling votes should ensure that payments for services rendered are settled within the same financial year whenever due.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the Chief Executive's key duty to the Board" according to official standards?
options: Implementation of Board decisions and policies.
Reporting only to the supervising Minister.
Management of the Board's personal affairs.
questions.explanation: The Chief Executive is accountable to the Board for implementing its approved decisions and policies.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under the Principle of Accountability, public officers are personally accountable for what?
options: Their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties.
The actions of the government as a whole. □.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties.). The Principle of Accountability states that public officers must be personally accountable for their individual actions or omissions in the performance of their duties, and must respond, where necessary, to their superiors.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under the cited rule, what share of recruitment must be reserved for persons with disabilities?
questions.explanation: The rule cited in the item requires that five percent of recruitment be reserved for persons with disabilities. The concept being tested is the specific quota set by the rule.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the Accountant-General's inspection duty intended to achieve" according to official standards?
options: To guarantee compliance with rules, regulations, and policy decisions.
To manage all federal government investments.
To issue warnings to defaulting officers.
questions.explanation: Routine and in-depth inspection of books of accounts is meant to guarantee compliance with rules, regulations, and policy decisions across public offices.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: How should deposits held in foreign currency be treated under the rules?
options: As the sole duty of the Central Bank of Nigeria.
As deposits outside the regulations entirely.
By converting them immediately to local currency.
In the same manner as deposits in local currency.
questions.explanation: Foreign-currency deposits are still subject to the same control logic as local-currency deposits unless a specific rule provides otherwise.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does the Board of Survey report show about its members' responsibility?
options: Only the Chairman is accountable for the report accuracy.
They are not accountable for the contents of the report.
They are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report.
They are responsible only for the physical counting.
questions.explanation: Members of the Board of Survey are jointly accountable for the accuracy of the report because all of them sign it and stand behind its contents.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What remains true after a Board of Survey finding for the officer in charge?
questions.explanation: The officer in charge remains accountable for discrepancies disclosed by the Board of Survey until the matter is satisfactorily resolved.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of these is an exclusive responsibility of the Federal Government?
questions.explanation: Foreign Affairs is an exclusive responsibility of the Federal Government. The item therefore tests recognition of a matter reserved to the Federal tier rather than a shared or concurrent public-service function.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "an example of non-tax revenue accruing to government" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (Operating surplus from its parastatals). The non-tax revenue refers to income accruable to the government outside its taxes such as operating surplus from its parastatals.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which two arms of the Federal Government share accountability for the Federal Budget?
options: The President and the Chief Justice
questions.explanation: The Executive prepares and implements the Federal Budget while the Legislature authorizes and oversees it, so both arms share accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What fiscal rule applies under the Fiscal Responsibility framework?
questions.explanation: A core fiscal-responsibility rule is that total expenditure should not exceed total revenue.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does the Manpower Development Office (MDO) coordinate on a service-wide basis?
options: The pool system for senior officers.
Training and development across the Civil Service.
Draft Council memoranda on HRM matters.
questions.explanation: The MDO coordinates service-wide training and development so that the Civil Service maintains the quality manpower needed for effective performance.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should a civil servant who has served for a very long time in one ministry avoid?
questions.explanation: Long service should not lead an officer to assume experience alone is sufficient, because institutional learning still requires openness and discipline.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should adjustments to the way services are organized and operated achieve?
questions.explanation: Adjustments to the way services are organized and operated should lead to better service delivery, supported by best practices and effective use of information and communication technologies.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does responsiveness in service delivery mean?
options: Serving only those who ask for help directly.
Delaying services as long as possible.
Serving stakeholders within a reasonable timeframe.
questions.explanation: Responsiveness in service delivery means meeting the needs of stakeholders within a reasonable and dependable timeframe.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who should receive copies of a handing-over note?
options: The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
questions.explanation: Copies of a handing-over note should be sent to the Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor to support continuity and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should guide the workflow for creating a new file?
options: The opinion of the Head of Department. Q.
questions.explanation: New files should be created based on the existing File Index so the numbering system stays consistent and logically ordered.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the purpose of a memorandum in the Civil Service" according to official standards?
options: To provide a confidential report only.
To communicate internally within the same department or Ministry.
questions.explanation: A memorandum is used for internal communication within the same department or Ministry. The item therefore tests recognition of the correspondence form used for internal official communication rather than public-facing or personal exchange.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who heads departments below the Permanent Secretary and is directly accountable to the Permanent Secretary?
questions.explanation: Directors head departments below the Permanent Secretary and are directly accountable to the Permanent Secretary.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: Who is the administrative head of a ministry?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Minister is the political head of a ministry, while the Permanent Secretary serves as the administrative head.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: Who is the chief adviser to the Minister and also the ministry's Accounting Officer?
options: The Chairman of the extra-ministerial department.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary serves as the chief adviser to the Minister and is also the ministry's Accounting Officer.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the designation of the topmost career civil servant in a local government" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: In a local government set-up, the topmost career civil servant is designated as the Secretary.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: What personnel role does the Board of a parastatal perform?
options: It acts as the sole disciplinary body for all staff.
It manages the day-to-day recruitment of junior staff.
It manages payroll and salaries directly.
It approves the appointment and promotion of staff.
questions.explanation: A parastatal board approves the appointment and promotion of staff, so it plays a key personnel oversight role rather than day-to-day administration.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: What function does a parastatal primarily perform in government administration?
options: To manage all human-resource issues for a ministry.
To implement specific government policies and programmes assigned to it.
To act as the legislative body for a ministry.
To assist ministries in policy formulation only.
questions.explanation: A parastatal exists mainly to implement specific government policies and programmes assigned to it.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: The disciplinary process for civil servants in the Nigerian Public Service is outlined in which chapter of the **PSR**'
questions.explanation: Chapter 10 of the PSR covers discipline, so that is the correct chapter for the disciplinary process.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "usually the first step in the disciplinary procedure for misconduct" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: The disciplinary process normally begins with the issuance of a written query, as reflected in PSR 100307.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the misconduct involving lateness, insubordination, negligence, or inappropriate dressing is classified as?
questions.explanation: Improper behavior like insubordination or habitual lateness is defined as Misconduct (PSR 100301).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which offence is classified as **Serious Misconduct** and may lead to dismissal?
options: Fraud, corruption, or unauthorized disclosure of official information.
Failure to attend an optional training course.
questions.explanation: Serious offences such as fraud, corruption, or the unauthorized disclosure of official information amount to Serious Misconduct under PSR 100401.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Falsification of official records constitutes which level of misconduct under the Civil Service Handbook
questions.explanation: Falsification of official records is treated as gross misconduct because it undermines trust, accountability, and the integrity of public records.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who is absent from duty without permission for 14 consecutive days may be deemed by the service to have?
questions.explanation: Unauthorized absence for 14 consecutive days is construed as resignation from the service (PSR 030413, implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer under **Suspension** pending investigation into serious misconduct receives what salary status
questions.explanation: Suspension (pending case conclusion for gross offenses) is usually without salary (PSR 100406, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which penalty for financial misconduct is intended to recover the exact amount of financial loss caused by the officer's negligence?
questions.explanation: Surcharge is the recovery of government losses from the officer's pay, applied when the loss is due to the officer’s fault.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the disciplinary action for misconduct may require an internal investigation by a **board of inquiry** consisting of at least?
questions.explanation: Allegations of misconduct are typically investigated by a Board of Inquiry with at least three members to ensure impartiality and fairness (Implied due process requirement).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does insubordination mean in the civil service?
options: Refusing to obey lawful instructions from a superior officer.
Reporting late for duty on one occasion.
Applying for promotion through the proper channel.
questions.explanation: Insubordination means refusing to obey a lawful instruction from a superior officer. It is treated as a misconduct issue because it undermines lawful authority and disciplined service conduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the ultimate disciplinary penalty for serious misconduct resulting in permanent removal and forfeiture of benefits is?
questions.explanation: Dismissal is the ultimate penalty for serious misconduct, resulting in permanent removal and forfeiture of most benefits.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** **rule 100409** mandates that an officer charged with a criminal offense must promptly report the charge to?
options: The Director of Finance and Accounts in the Ministry.
His Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
The Chair of the Ministerial Disciplinary Committee.
questions.explanation: An Officer must promptly report a criminal charge to his Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office (PSR 100409, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is prohibited as a disciplinary punishment under the **PSR**'?
questions.explanation: The PSR explicitly prohibits the imposition of fines as a disciplinary punishment (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: An officer under **Interdiction** receives what salary status
questions.explanation: During interdiction (temporary suspension pending investigation), an officer is placed on half pay (PSR 100404, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the intentional act of using government vehicles or property for private business is classified as?
questions.explanation: Using government property, such as vehicles, for private business is considered gross misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the penalty for an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts (**PSR** 030104)?
options: Required to resign or be withdrawn from the service.
questions.explanation: An Officer who fails the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to resign or be withdrawn from the service (PSR 030104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the contravention of the prohibition on membership of a secret society (**psr** 020211) is regarded as?
questions.explanation: Contravention of the prohibition against secret societies is regarded as an act of serious misconduct (PSR 020212, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body holds the final authority on matters of discipline and appeal for senior civil servants (**GL 07** and above)?
options: The Office of the Head of Civil Service (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The FCSC has final authority on appointment, promotion, and discipline for senior officers.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an aggrieved officer appealing a disciplinary decision (chapter 11) must generally have the appeal handled within a maximum period of?
questions.explanation: An appeal submitted more than six months after the decision will generally not be entertained unless the delay is explained (PSR 110209(b)), implying a six-month window.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which item is not a valid ground for rejecting a petition or appeal under PSR 110208?
options: A pending court case on the same matter.
Inclusion of the required signature and staff number.
Repetition of earlier claims without new facts.
questions.explanation: A petition should be accepted when it includes the required authentication details such as signature and staff number; rejection grounds include illegibility, pending litigation, repetition without new facts, or similar defects.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the under **psr** **rule 040202**, an officer's increment may be deferred or withheld on grounds of?
options: Unsatisfactory performance or being under interdiction/suspension.
questions.explanation: An increment shall normally be granted unless the officer is on interdiction/suspension or has a disciplinary action pending, or on account of poor performance (PSR 040202).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the engaging in **partisan political activities** is classified as?
questions.explanation: Engaging in partisan political activities is listed as Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(f), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an **MDA** discovers new misconduct during an ongoing disciplinary inquiry, what must happen before punishment can be applied
options: The officer must be dismissed immediately.
The new offense must be ignored.
The officer must be informed and allowed to respond to the new allegation.
The Board of Inquiry must be disbanded.
questions.explanation: The officer must be informed and given the opportunity to defend themselves (implied by due process and natural justice, PSR 100307).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes **General Inefficiency** (**PSR** **Rule 100201**)?
options: A pattern of omissions or incompetence showing inability to perform duties.
Refusal to obey a lawful order.
questions.explanation: General Inefficiency consists of a series of omissions or incompetence, the cumulative effect of which shows that the Officer is not capable of discharging efficiently the duties of the office he holds (PSR 100201).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the in deciding whether to withhold or defer an increment (**psr** 040204), the permanent secretary must take into account?
options: The gravity of the shortcoming and the quality of the officer's previous service.
The size of the MDA's annual budget.
questions.explanation: The decision must weigh the gravity of the shortcoming against the quality of the officer's previous service, as withholding is a serious penalty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** forbids officers from seeking to influence prominent persons to gain promotion or transfer (**psr** 100427), aiming to enforce the principle of?
questions.explanation: PSR 100427 prohibits seeking influence to ensure appointments and promotions are based on merit.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the misconduct involving the use of official information for personal financial advantage is prohibited under the ethical rule of?
questions.explanation: Officers must avoid conflicts of interest, such as using official information for personal gain Reference: rule of.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who has the authority to issue a written query to a Director (**GL 17**) in a Ministry?
options: The Head of the Procurement Unit.
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary, as the administrative head of the Ministry, is the proper authority to issue a written query to a Director.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body is responsible for enforcing ethical standards in public office by receiving and verifying public officers’ **asset declarations**'?
questions.explanation: The Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB) receives and verifies public officers’ asset declarations (Constitutional mandate).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the maximum period for the deferment of an officer's increment must not exceed?
questions.explanation: The period for deferred increment must not be less than three months nor more than six months (PSR 040205),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the main if an officer is required to sign a **bond** for specialized training, the purpose is to ensure?
options: The officer receives automatic promotion after training.
They do not retire before completing the bond service commitment.
The officer can accept external employment during the bond period.
The training institution assumes all post-training deployment decisions.
questions.explanation: Bonds (PSR 070205) are required for specialized training to ensure service commitment upon completion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under PSR 070210, what happens if a female officer interrupts a training course of not more than six months because of pregnancy?
options: She may be required to refund all or part of the course cost.
questions.explanation: PSR 070210 provides that where such a short training course is interrupted on grounds of pregnancy, the officer may be required to refund all or part of the course cost.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the prohibition on receiving or granting **preferential treatment** (nepotism) in appointments is classified as?
questions.explanation: Nepotism is listed as an act of Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(t), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the under the **psr**, an officer's discipline can be applied to?
options: Only work-related conduct during official hours.
Both work-related and off-duty conduct that affects the service.
questions.explanation: Discipline can apply to both work-related and off-duty conduct that affects the service (Implied scope of PSR Chapter 10).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which officer is empowered to initiate disciplinary action by issuing a query for misconduct?
options: The officer's supervisor or Head of Department.
questions.explanation: A supervisor, Head of Department, or other appropriate disciplinary superior may initiate disciplinary action by issuing a query for misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: If an officer's salary is withheld because of unauthorized absence, which **PSR** rule governs that sanction?
questions.explanation: PSR 040206 specifically provides for the withholding of salary in cases of unauthorized absence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the imposition of progressive discipline for repeated minor offenses typically starts with?
questions.explanation: Repeated minor offenses attract progressive discipline, starting with warnings (Implied procedural requirement).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** requires that officers avoid all forms of **sexual harassment**, classified under?
questions.explanation: Sexual harassment is strictly prohibited under the Code of Conduct and is a serious disciplinary matter (PSR Chapter 10 context).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who must approve a petition before it is considered by the appropriate authority (**PSR** practice)?
options: The officer's immediate supervisor (through proper channels).
questions.explanation: Complaints/grievances must be submitted through the appropriate channel, usually starting with the immediate superior (PSR 020606),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Through what channel may an officer appeal a disciplinary decision under the PSR?
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals).). Aggrieved officers can petition decisions through the proper channels as described in Chapter 11 (Petitions and Appeals),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer under investigation may be transferred to another **mda**. this action is taken under the authority of?
questions.explanation: Officers under investigation may be transferred, subject to FCSC consultation (PSR 110205 context).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the period for which a deferred increment must be fixed is not less than three months nor more than?
questions.explanation: The deferment period must not be less than three months nor more than six months (PSR 040205),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: The **PSR** prohibits the use of which platform for official communication
questions.explanation: Official communication must follow hierarchical channels; social media is generally prohibited for official correspondence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** requires that all financial misconduct leading to dismissal must be supported by?
options: A Board of Inquiry report and internal investigation findings.
questions.explanation: Dismissal for misconduct must be supported by investigation findings following due process (PSR 100305, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When an officer is charged with Misconduct, the disciplinary query must contain?
options: Details of the offense, the broken rule, and the likely penalty.
A list of all previous employers.
questions.explanation: The query must be precise, relating to the circumstances of the offense, the rule broken, and the likely penalty (PSR 100307).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the the **psr** rule that mandates officers cannot be punished without being heard ensures adherence to the principle of?
questions.explanation: The right to defend oneself (PSR 100307) ensures natural justice is upheld.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which agency is primarily responsible for investigating and prosecuting serious corruption cases involving public officers?
questions.explanation: The EFCC (Economic and Financial Crimes Commission) investigates and prosecutes financial crimes and corruption cases.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the prohibition on **holding more than one full-time paid job** (**psr** 100402(r)) is classified as?
questions.explanation: Holding more than one full-time paid job is listed as an act of Serious Misconduct (PSR 100402(r), implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Disciplinary action resulting in **Demotion** (Reduction in Rank) is listed under which category of sanctions
questions.explanation: Demotion (Reduction in Rank) is one of the penalties prescribed under the disciplinary measures outlined in PSR Chapter 10 (Implied sanction).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the final authority to discipline directors (**gl 17**) in the federal civil service rests with the?
options: Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission is the disciplinary authority for Directors in the Federal Civil Service, so it is the correct answer.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens governance in discipline and conduct administration?
options: Approved procedures with complete case records.
questions.explanation: Governance in discipline and conduct administration is strongest when officers follow the approved procedure and keep complete case records for review and accountability.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective discipline & conduct, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure discipline & conduct compliance
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
questions.explanation: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step transparently. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which step best helps a supervisor detect discipline risks before they become formal misconduct cases?
options: Early identification and escalation of warning signs.
questions.explanation: Supervisors reduce misconduct risk when warning signs are identified early, escalated properly, and followed up before the matter becomes formal.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper administrative ethics standards?
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice should a responsible officer prioritize to sustain grievance handling in Discipline & Conduct?
options: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Resolve complaints using fair, timely, and documented procedures. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates performance management
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best sustains discipline and conduct standards in a public-service unit?
options: Consistent compliance with approved conduct standards.
Informal practice instead of approved process.
questions.explanation: Discipline and conduct standards are sustained when officers apply approved controls consistently instead of relying on informal discretion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option most strongly aligns with good public-service practice on anti-corruption safeguards within Discipline & Conduct?
options: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Escalate suspicious practices through proper reporting channels. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports documented procedure in discipline and conduct administration?
options: Complete records kept under the approved procedure.
questions.explanation: Documented procedure depends on following the approved process and keeping complete records of the steps taken.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best strengthens compliance assurance when handling a suspected breach of conduct?
options: Apply the approved rules consistently and escalate exceptions that need higher review.
Make ad hoc exceptions whenever the officer involved has a good prior record.
Delay action until several unrelated complaints have accumulated.
Close the matter once an informal explanation sounds plausible.
questions.explanation: Compliance assurance depends on applying the approved rules consistently and escalating exceptions that require higher review. That keeps conduct handling fair, reviewable, and aligned with the service rules.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which action best demonstrates public accountability in discipline and conduct administration?
questions.explanation: Public accountability depends on decisions that can be traced to recorded reasons and supporting evidence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective discipline & conduct, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure service integrity
options: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
questions.explanation: Avoid conflicts of interest and disclose relevant constraints. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When a conduct complaint is received, which control best reduces the risk of a flawed disciplinary process?
options: Documented review with fair hearing and fact verification.
questions.explanation: A disciplinary process is less likely to be flawed when the complaint is reviewed through documented steps, fair hearing, and fact verification.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper decision transparency standards?
options: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Use clear criteria and communicate decisions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which routine best sustains operational discipline in a unit that handles sensitive official work?
options: Completion of approved workflow checks before closure.
questions.explanation: Operational discipline depends on completing approved workflow checks before a matter is closed or advanced.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates citizen-focused service
options: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Balance legality, fairness, timeliness, and service quality. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports record management in discipline and conduct administration?
options: Current files with status updates at each control point.
questions.explanation: Record management depends on keeping files current and updating status at each control point so the matter remains reviewable.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which approach best supports performance standards in a discipline-sensitive public-service unit?
options: Set measurable expectations, monitor progress, and correct deviations before they become habitual.
Assume standards will be understood without defining them in practical terms.
Ignore repeated slippage so long as the unit meets a final deadline.
Use different standards for similar officers based on convenience.
questions.explanation: Performance standards are strongest when expectations are measurable, progress is monitored, and deviations are corrected early. That prevents weak practice from hardening into a discipline problem.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which routine best strengthens governance standards in discipline and conduct administration?
options: Approved governance procedures with complete records.
questions.explanation: Governance standards are strongest when officers follow the approved procedure consistently and keep the records needed for review and continuity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best preserves transparency when disciplinary decisions are taken?
options: Use lawful criteria and document each decision step and its justification clearly.
Rely on informal instructions so the process can move faster.
Treat undocumented exceptions as acceptable when the outcome seems fair.
Close the matter without recording how the decision was reached.
questions.explanation: Transparency is preserved when disciplinary decisions are based on lawful criteria and each step is documented with a clear justification. That makes the process easier to review and defend.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In the context of Discipline & Conduct, which action best demonstrates discipline & conduct risk management
options: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly.
Prioritize convenience over policy and legal requirements.
Ignore feedback and continue non-compliant procedures.
Apply rules inconsistently based on personal preference.
questions.explanation: Identify control gaps early and escalate material exceptions promptly. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: For effective discipline & conduct, what is the most appropriate approach to ensure administrative ethics
options: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism.
Close cases without validating facts or required records.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
questions.explanation: Uphold neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports grievance handling within discipline and conduct administration?
options: Prompt review through fair and documented complaint channels.
Personal preference instead of approved review channels.
questions.explanation: Grievance handling is strongest when complaints are reviewed promptly through fair, documented channels and followed up properly.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When handling discipline & conduct, which choice reflects proper performance management standards?
options: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles.
Rely on informal instructions without documentary evidence.
Delay decisions until issues escalate into avoidable crises.
Treat exceptions as routine without documented justification.
questions.explanation: Use objective indicators and structured feedback cycles. It strengthens compliance, consistency, and accountability in discipline & conduct Reference: Discipline & Conduct - Expansion Set.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best sustains discipline and conduct in daily official work?
options: Consistent compliance with approved conduct standards.
Informal practice instead of approved process.
questions.explanation: Daily discipline and conduct depend on consistent compliance with approved standards rather than convenience or informal shortcuts.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: How does the attendance register distinguish staff who arrive on time from those who arrive late?
options: The register draws a red line, and latecomers sign below it.
The register gets a note beside each late name.
The register lets everyone sign in exactly the same place without distinction.
The register sends latecomers to a separate attendance sheet.
questions.explanation: The attendance register uses a red line drawn at the prescribed time so late arrivals sign below it and the office can distinguish punctuality from lateness.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is the filed original of the Handing Over Notes passed to the supervisor or a superior officer?
options: To guarantee that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.
To allow them to rewrite it.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (To guarantee that the duties of that post have been properly taken over.). The filed original should then be passed to the Supervisor and/or a superior officer who should know that the duties of that post have been properly taken over. 491
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: With whom must an officer not communicate on official business outside the proper channel unless directed by a superior officer?
options: A junior officer in the same office.
A more senior officer in the same ministry.
questions.explanation: An officer must not communicate on official business with a non-official or non-government person outside the proper channel unless specifically directed to do so by a superior officer.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: In official correspondence, what does the term 'minute/memo' mean?
options: A short report for a supervisor.
A written submission presenting a view or position on an issue.
questions.explanation: A minute or memo is a written submission used to present a view, comment, or position on an issue within official correspondence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Is a member of an Examination Board automatically entitled to invigilator or examiner fees?
options: Yes, as long as they are a director.
No, only those who perform the duties of invigilation or examination are eligible.
The rules are silent on this.
questions.explanation: No. A member of an Examination Board is not automatically entitled to invigilator or examiner fees unless the person actually performs the duties of invigilation or examining. The item therefore distinguishes Board membership from paid examination work.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which role is the topmost career Civil Servant in a Local Government set-up?
questions.explanation: The topmost career civil servant in a local government set-up is the Secretary, which is why that option is correct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: According to the handbook, how should a ministry treat a newly joining civil servant?
options: Subject them to a probationary period without any support.
Give them the most difficult tasks to test their resolve. □.
Ignore them and let them learn on their own.
Receive them with kindness and courtesy, and assist them to settle down.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (Receive them with kindness and courtesy, and assist them to settle down.). New Officers joining a Ministry should be received with kindness and courtesy and should be assisted in every way to settle down.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What risk arises when a civil servant accepts gifts from contractors or business people?
questions.explanation: Accepting gifts from contractors or business people can compromise impartiality and damage the civil servant's integrity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does the principle of diligence require of a civil servant?
options: To be hardworking and conscientious, and to give their best to the job.
To delegate all tasks to subordinates.
To work only when closely supervised.
questions.explanation: The principle of diligence requires a civil servant to be hardworking, conscientious, and committed to giving their best to the job.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the implication of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a civil servant" according to official standards?
options: It is a normal part of the job.
It is only a minor infraction.
questions.explanation: A civil servant is expected to take reasonable care of official documents. Failure to do so constitutes an offence.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should civil servants avoid when dealing with the public?
options: Asking for gifts or favors. Q.
Being helpful, but still being impartial.
questions.explanation: Correct option: A (Asking for gifts or favors. Q. 432). It is a disgrace for a Civil Servant to tout for official matters and demand money or gifts from members of the public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should be done when a file will need further action later?
options: Passing the file without recording the transfer.
questions.explanation: A file that will require later action should be placed on a bring-up pad for the relevant date and returned through the proper registry process.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which document is used for formal communication with other Ministries, parastatals, and the public?
questions.explanation: An official letter is used for formal communication with other Ministries, parastatals, and the public. A memorandum is for internal communication within the same ministry or department.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the consequence of failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a civil servant" according to official standards?
options: It is a normal part of the job.
It is a minor infraction only.
questions.explanation: Failing to take reasonable care of documents entrusted to a civil servant is an offense. The rule protects official records from loss, misuse, or unauthorized exposure by making careless handling a disciplinary matter.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What are civil servants advised not to leave on their desks at the end of the day?
questions.explanation: Civil servants are advised not to leave official files and documents on their desks at the end of the day. That precaution helps prevent loss, misplacement, or unauthorized access to official records.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who should a civil servant inform first when a file has stayed on the desk too long without action?
questions.explanation: The first step is to inform the superior officer so the delay can be escalated through the proper administrative chain.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should a civil servant do if an assigned file has remained on the desk too long without action?
options: Report the delay to a superior officer and return the file for proper registry control.
Keep the file until someone asks about it.
Pass it to a colleague without recording the transfer.
Dispose of it to reduce the backlog.
questions.explanation: A file that has stayed too long without action should be reported to a superior officer and returned for proper registry control so accountability is restored.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main purpose of an official letter in the Civil Service" according to official standards?
options: To communicate formally with other Ministries, parastatals, and the public.
To serve as a confidential report to be kept off the record.
To record a private conversation between officers.
To replace internal memoranda used within one department.
questions.explanation: The main purpose of an official letter is formal external communication. It is used when a ministry or department communicates with other Ministries, parastatals, agencies, or members of the public.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is treated as a very serious offense for a civil servant?
options: Unauthorized disclosure of information acquired in the course of duty.
questions.explanation: Unauthorized disclosure of information acquired in the course of duty is treated as a very serious offense for a civil servant. The question therefore tests recognition of a grave breach of official trust and confidentiality.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which conduct is considered a very serious offense for a civil servant?
options: Unauthorized disclosure of information acquired in the course of duty.
questions.explanation: Unauthorized disclosure of information acquired in the course of duty is considered a very serious offense. The item therefore tests the seriousness attached to breaching official confidentiality in the public service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which form of communication is an official letter used for in the Civil Service?
options: To record a personal conversation between officers.
To communicate with other Ministries, parastatals, and the public.
To serve as a confidential report for internal circulation only.
To replace an internal memorandum within one department.
questions.explanation: An official letter is used for formal communication with other Ministries, parastatals, agencies, and the public. It is different from a memorandum, which is used for communication within the same department or ministry.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should a civil servant do with a circular that demands action?
options: Place a copy in the relevant file and pass it to the appropriate officer for action.
questions.explanation: Correct option: C (Place a copy in the relevant file and pass it to the appropriate officer for action.). The file copy of each such circular should be placed in the file relevant to the subject matter and should be passed to the appropriate officer for action to be taken.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is considered a very serious offence for a Civil Servant?
options: Taking a casual day off without prior notice.
Unauthorised disclosure of information acquired in the course of duty.
Talking to a colleague during office hours.
questions.explanation: Unauthorised disclosure of information obtained in the course of duty is treated as a very serious offence because it breaches the trust and confidentiality expected of a Civil Servant.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the final authority on matters of ethical conduct and compliance with the **psr**, particularly concerning disciplinary appeals, rests with the?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The Civil Service Commission has ultimate authority on matters of ethical conduct and appeals.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: What disciplinary role should a supervisor perform in a registry?
options: Enforce discipline with justice and fairness at all times.
Allow staff to do as they please.
Prevent staff from ever leaving the office.
questions.explanation: A registry supervisor should enforce discipline with justice and fairness so that office procedures are followed properly.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

questions.question: How should a registry supervisor enforce discipline?
options: By preventing staff from leaving the office.
By allowing staff to do as they please.
By doing all the work personally.
With justice and fairness at all times.
questions.explanation: Discipline in a registry should be enforced with justice and fairness so that staff are managed properly and procedures are respected.
questions.sourceDocument: Civil Service Handbook

subcategories.name: Performance & Training
subcategories.description: Performance systems, training, and capacity development.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the primary tool used for the annual evaluation of a civil servant’s competence and performance is the?
questions.explanation: The Annual Performance Evaluation Report (APER/PAF) is the official tool used to evaluate staff performance annually (PSR 050402).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Promotion eligibility is primarily based on which three key factors, according to the **PSR**'
options: Merit, performance, and availability of vacancy.
Personal wealth, age, and foreign training.
Attendance, handwriting, and social media activity.
questions.explanation: Promotion eligibility is based on merit, performance, and availability of vacancy, and absence of disciplinary issues (PSR 020802(d)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under which PSR rule is the standard incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted in the Federal Public Service set out?
questions.explanation: PSR Rule 040203 sets out the standard incremental date for an officer appointed or promoted in the Federal Public Service. It is the specific rule that governs how the incremental date is fixed in that situation.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the **Performance Management Cycle** running from January to December of every year (**PSR** 050301)?
options: To calculate the annual leave roster.
To ensure staff performance aligns with MDA institutional goals and targets.
To determine the final retirement age.
questions.explanation: The Performance Management Cycle runs from January to December to align individual and institutional goals and targets.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under PSR 070105, which category of training is a long-term, fully sponsored postgraduate study considered crucial to the officer and the MDA's mandate?
questions.explanation: Category A under PSR 070105 is the long-term, fully sponsored postgraduate training considered crucial and beneficial both to the officer and to the MDA's mandate.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer who fails the compulsory confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts shall be required to?
options: Be promoted to the next grade level.
Resign or be withdrawn from the service.
Be transferred to a new MDA.
questions.explanation: Failure to pass the confirmation examination after three consecutive attempts requires the officer to resign or be withdrawn from the service (PSR 030104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the **psr** permits officers to participate in courses of instruction within nigeria, provided the course is conducted by?
options: Any private consultancy that issues attendance certificates.
Any Federal Training Centre, University or approved Public Service Training institution.
Informal apprenticeship arranged by the officer's department.
Political party training secretariats recognized by local chapters.
questions.explanation: Courses of instruction within Nigeria must be conducted by a Federal Training Centre, University, or an approved Public Service Training institution (PSR 070104).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What may happen to an officer's increment while the officer is under interdiction or suspension?
questions.explanation: Under the PSR, an officer under interdiction or suspension may have the normal increment withheld or deferred.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the period of deferred increment (**psr** 040205) must not be less than three months nor more than?
questions.explanation: The deferment period must not be less than three months nor more than six months,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "true when a withheld increment is restored" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: A withheld increment is normally restored prospectively; the rule does not call for automatic retrospective restoration.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the training strategy (**psr** 070103) aims to ensure continuous capacity development shall be encapsulated in the?
options: Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy.
questions.explanation: Strategies to ensure continuous capacity development shall be encapsulated in the Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy,.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Promotion examinations are typically required for officers on which range of Grade Levels
questions.explanation: Promotion examinations are typically required for officers on grade levels 07 to 15 (Implied PSR policy).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the if an officer is promoted to a grade level where the new emolument point is higher than the minimum point of the new salary grade level, they shall be placed at the?
options: Minimum point of the new grade level irrespective of former emolument.
Next salary point higher than their former emolument.
Nearest lower point on the new grade to avoid pay distortion.
Mid-point of the new salary grade pending confirmation.
questions.explanation: If the emolument in the former post is higher than the minimum point of the new salary grade level, the officer will be placed on the next point, higher than their former emolument (PSR 040104(ii)).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body has the final authority over the syllabus and guidelines for all promotion examinations?
options: Office of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (OHCSF).
questions.explanation: The Federal Civil Service Commission has the final authority to issue the syllabus and guidelines for promotion examinations under PSR 020802(a).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** **rule 070101** defines staff development as the policy of enhancing the knowledge, skills, attitude, effectiveness, and efficiency of staff to meet?
options: Short-term political demands and campaign assignments.
Career goals and prepare them for changing duties and responsibilities.
Personal income targets outside official service objectives.
Union executive ambitions unrelated to organizational needs.
questions.explanation: Staff development aims to meet career goals and prepare staff for changing duties and responsibilities, Reference: Rule 070101.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the for a newly appointed officer, emoluments (salary) shall generally be paid from the date of?
questions.explanation: Rule 040102 confirms that emoluments shall, as a general rule, be paid as from the date of assumption of duty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Promotion arrears under **PSR** 040104(iii) are calculated from which date?
options: Date of the new salary scale commencement.
Date the officer passed the promotion examination.
questions.explanation: Under PSR 040104(iii), promotion arrears are calculated from the notional date of promotion.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer may be granted special leave with full pay (**psr** 120222) to take a **non-compulsory** examination, provided the permanent secretary certifies that the passing of the examination is likely to?
options: Aid the officer's transfer to a foreign service.
Enhance his value to the Service.
Grant him a private business license.
questions.explanation: Leave for Non-Compulsory Examination requires certification that passing the exam is likely to enhance his value to the Service (PSR 120222).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under the **PSR**, within what maximum period must all officers be placed on the **IPPIS** platform after assumption of duty?
options: Two months of assumption of duty.
questions.explanation: PSR 040102 requires every officer to be placed on the IPPIS platform within two months of assumption of duty.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which body ensures promotion guidelines are followed by **MDAs** and sets definite promotion criteria?
questions.explanation: The FCSC sets definite promotion criteria and guidelines, ensuring uniformity and meritocracy.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule stipulates that an officer who fails to take the confirmation examination after 3 years of first appointment is required to resign?
questions.explanation: Rule 030105 requires officers who fail to take the confirmation examination after 3 years of first appointment to resign.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the an officer may be required to serve in **any part of the country**. this principle emphasizes?
questions.explanation: Officers may be required to serve in any part of the country, reflecting the principle of National Service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary training opportunities must be equitably distributed to all staff in **mdas**, as per the objectives of?
questions.explanation: The Public Service Training and Capacity Development Policy guarantees equitable training access to all staff (PSR 070103),.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a reference book allowed for use during the confirmation examination for senior officers (**GL 07** and above)?
options: Textbooks and reference books related to the General Paper.
questions.explanation: Textbooks and reference books are not allowed for the examinations in the General Paper or office Procedure/Routine or Special Paper (PSR 030107).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following describes the the authority to approve the restoration of a previously deferred increment rests with the?
options: National Salaries, Incomes and Wages Commission (NSIWC).
questions.explanation: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office holds the authority to decide on restoring a deferred increment (PSR 040208, implied).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who must approve the release of any officer on study leave (**PSR** 120231)?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation.
questions.explanation: The release of any Officer on study leave shall be subject to the approval of the Head of the Civil Service of the Federation (PSR 120231).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which **PSR** rule allows for the possibility of special increments to mitigate the negative effects of a withheld increment?
questions.explanation: Rule 040209 provides that the FCSC may grant one or more special increments to mitigate the effects of withholding increments (Implied rule reference).
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What is the primary the primary objective of the **reward and recognition system (r&rs)** in the civil service is to?
options: Institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff.
Simplify the process of staff dismissal.
questions.explanation: The R&RS aims to institutionalize a culture of excellence and motivate staff by recognizing meritorious service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best explains the **psr** **rule 070210** requires a female officer who interrupts a course of training of **not more than six months duration** on grounds of pregnancy to?
options: Refund the whole or part of the cost of the course.
Be transferred to a non-training post.
questions.explanation: A female officer who interrupts a course of training of not more than six months duration shall be called upon to refund the whole or part of the cost of the course, Reference: Rule 070210.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who is responsible for ensuring the payment of the travel expenses, lodging, and sundries for an officer on Sabbatical Appointment?
