questions.question: Which duty belongs to the Accountant-General regarding accounting systems and controls?

questions.question: What rule best describes the Sub-Accounting Officer's role in revenue collection?
options: Delegation of the entire duty to junior staff.
Responsibility only for cash physically received.
No formal role in revenue collection.
Accountability for departmental control and collection of revenue.
questions.explanation: The Sub-Accounting Officer remains accountable for departmental control and collection of revenue under the relevant financial rules.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Under Financial Regulation 230(iii), how should arrears of revenue that remain outstanding from previous years be treated?
options: They should be included in the current year's arrears of revenue returns.
They should be reported separately to the Minister of Finance.
They should be automatically written off.
They remain the sole duty of the previous Accounting Officer.
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 230(iii) requires arrears of revenue returns to include all arrears still outstanding from previous years unless they have been formally written off.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Can an officer with delegated expenditure control sub-delegate that duty?
options: Yes, but only with the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote.
questions.explanation: Delegated expenditure control may only be sub-delegated with the knowledge and approval of the officer controlling the vote; it is not a free or automatic power.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What duty do officers controlling votes owe regarding payment for services rendered?
questions.explanation: Officers controlling votes must ensure that payment for services rendered is settled within the same financial year whenever due.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What duty do Sub-Accounting Officers owe in relation to receipt and licence books?

Leaving the books unsecured until required.
Safe custody and proper use of the books.
questions.explanation: Sub-Accounting Officers must keep receipt and licence books in safe custody and ensure they are properly used.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What type of control is emphasized for the collection of all revenues under the duty of the Accounting Officer?
questions.explanation: Financial Regulation 1603(g) emphasizes the need for an adequate system of internal control over the collection of all revenues under the Accounting Officer's responsibility.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who bears primary accountability for discrepancies in financial records within a self-accounting unit?

questions.question: Which arm of government examines and approves the Federal Budget?

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "a key accountability of the Chief Executive of a parastatal to the Board" according to official standards?

questions.question: What does the Manpower Development Office coordinate across the Civil Service?
options: Service-wide administration of training and development.
The pool system for senior officers.
Preparation of draft Council memoranda on human resource matters.
questions.explanation: The Manpower Development Office coordinates service-wide training and development so that the Civil Service maintains the quality manpower it needs.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option correctly addresses: If the chairman and other members cannot agree on the aim of a meeting, what is the chairman's accountability?
options: To move on with the meeting anyway.
To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.
To force his own interpretation of the role on the members. Q.
questions.explanation: Correct option: B (To revert to the convening authority to seek clarification.). Where the chairman and the other members cannot achieve such an understanding or agreement on what the aim is, it is the chairman's duty to revert to the convening authority to seek and secure clarification.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: After a meeting closes, what should the secretary ensure?
options: The meeting record is ignored once discussion ends.
Responsibility is transferred informally to participants.
No further action is needed after attendance is recorded.
Minutes are written, circulated, and follow-up actions are completed.
questions.explanation: After a meeting, the secretary should ensure that the minutes are written and circulated and that agreed follow-up actions are completed.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: When decisions are made in civil service values and integrity work, which step most directly improves traceability and fairness?
options: Apply approved procedures consistently and document each material step.
Treat exceptions as normal practice without written justification.
Rely on verbal approval and close the file without a documentary trail.
Proceed without validating source records and decision criteria.
questions.explanation: Applying approved procedures consistently and documenting each material step is the best way to make decisions traceable and fair.
questions.chapter: Civil Service Values & Integrity - Expansion Set
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does the Manpower Development Office (MDO) coordinate in the Civil Service?
options: Preparation of draft Council memoranda on HRM matters.
Deployment of staff to every ministry.
The pool system for senior officers.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of conflict of interest in the civil service?
questions.explanation: Conflict of interest occurs when a public officer awards contracts or takes decisions for personal benefit.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which misconduct involves using an official position to obtain undue advantage?
questions.explanation: Abuse of office occurs when an official uses their position for personal advantage or gain.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which misconduct involves using office resources for private business?
questions.explanation: Abuse of office includes using official resources for personal or private business.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which misconduct involves unauthorized use of official vehicles?
questions.explanation: Abuse of office is correct because unauthorized use of official vehicles is a misuse of public authority and resources.

Treating undeclared interests as harmless if the officer seems fair.
Leaving conflict decisions to personal judgment without a formal record.
questions.explanation: Conflict-of-interest governance depends on consistent disclosure rules, proper recusals, and a record that shows how the conflict was managed.

questions.question: Which control best helps an officer manage a conflict of interest before it compromises a public decision?

Participating fully and explaining the relationship only if someone later asks.

subcategories.name: Misconduct & Discipline

questions.question: Which of these is considered misconduct under the Public Service Rules?
questions.explanation: Corruption is classified as misconduct and attracts disciplinary measures under the Public Service Rules Reference: Rules.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which type of misconduct involves deliberate misrepresentation of facts?
questions.explanation: Falsehood is deliberate misrepresentation of facts, so it is treated as misconduct because it undermines honesty, trust, and proper official records.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which civil service rule prohibits acceptance of double remuneration?
questions.explanation: The Public Service Rules prohibit public officers from receiving double pay from government sources Reference: rule prohibits.

questions.question: Which misconduct involves divulging official secrets without authorization?
questions.explanation: Divulging official secrets without authorization breaches confidentiality and is serious misconduct.

questions.question: Which misconduct involves unauthorized absence from duty?
questions.explanation: Absenteeism is misconduct where a public officer fails to report to duty without permission.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which misconduct involves falsifying records for personal advantage?
questions.explanation: Forgery is correct because falsifying official records for personal advantage is a form of record fraud.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

subcategories.name: General Ethics
subcategories.description: General ethical reasoning for public officers.

questions.question: Which document sets out the duties, responsibilities and conduct of civil servants?
questions.explanation: The Public Service Rules set out terms and conditions, conduct, duties and responsibilities for civil servants.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does 'Estacode' relate to in civil service practice?
questions.explanation: Estacode is the set of rules on official travel and allowances in the civil/public service.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following is a best-practice principle for official correspondence retention?
options: Keep both incoming and outgoing official correspondence for the prescribed retention period.
Keep only electronic copies without backups.
questions.explanation: Good records management requires retention of official incoming and outgoing correspondence for specified retention schedules and proper archiving.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best demonstrates sound ethical governance in public service?
options: Applying approved ethical standards consistently and keeping a defensible record.
Changing standards from case to case based on convenience.
Treating personal judgment as a substitute for formal guidance.
questions.explanation: Ethical governance is strongest when approved standards are applied consistently and the reasons for sensitive decisions are properly recorded.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which daily work habit best helps a public officer keep routine decisions ethically sound?
options: Consistent use of approved rules and recorded reasons for sensitive decisions.
Personal instinct despite an available rule or procedure.
Case-by-case adjustment of ethical standards under pressure.
Acceptance of undocumented exceptions because the outcome seems useful.
questions.explanation: Routine decisions stay ethically sound when approved rules are applied consistently and reasons are recorded for decisions that may later require review.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports ethics risk management in public service?
options: Identifying ethical control gaps early and escalating serious exceptions promptly.
Waiting for misconduct to become public before addressing warning signs.
Treating repeated ethical lapses as normal under operational pressure.
Allowing undocumented departures from standards when the outcome looks beneficial.
questions.explanation: Ethics risk management depends on identifying control gaps early and escalating serious exceptions before routine lapses harden into misconduct.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which conduct best reflects proper ethical standards in everyday public service work?
options: Showing neutrality, integrity, and service professionalism in routine decisions.
Applying rules differently for friends, superiors, and strangers.
Keeping important ethical decisions off the official record.
Treating undocumented favours as harmless if no one complains.
questions.explanation: Everyday ethical conduct in public service depends on neutrality, integrity, and professionalism being maintained even in routine decisions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which practice best supports grievance handling in an ethics-sensitive workplace issue?
options: Addressing the complaint through fair, timely, and documented steps.
Closing the matter quickly so the office avoids embarrassment.
Treating the complaint as personal gossip rather than an official concern.
Ignoring breaches once the complainant stops following up.

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the term for a set of official correspondence and documents kept together on one subject" according to official standards?
questions.explanation: A file is the set of official correspondence and related documents kept together on a particular subject. It is the standard records unit used to organize, retrieve, and preserve office documents.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who should prepare a draft reply when official correspondence requires one?
questions.explanation: A schedule officer prepares a draft reply whenever the item of correspondence allocated to the officer requires a reply.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should every official letter contain?
options: A complete history of earlier correspondence.
A subject heading, reference number, and date.
questions.explanation: Every official letter should carry a heading that states the subject briefly together with a reference number and date for identification and record-keeping.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should letters always have?
options: A heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter, a reference number, and a date.
A list of all previous correspondence.
questions.explanation: Letters should always have a heading briefly descriptive of the subject matter and must bear a reference number and date.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Who prepares the draft reply to allocated official correspondence?
questions.explanation: Where official correspondence demands a reply, the schedule officer to whom it is allocated is responsible for preparing the draft reply.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should official letters always contain?
options: A heading briefly describing the subject matter, together with a reference number and date.
A list of all previous correspondence.
questions.explanation: Official letters should always carry a heading that briefly describes the subject matter, along with a reference number and date for proper identification and record-keeping.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why should letters always have a heading, reference number, and date?
options: To identify the subject and provide proper record control.
To make the letter longer and more formal-sounding.
To replace the need for filing and cross-reference.
To allow officers to omit the body of the letter.
questions.explanation: An official letter should carry a heading, reference number, and date so its subject can be identified easily and the record can be controlled properly.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "the main purpose of a secret registry" according to official standards?
options: To store records of open meetings.
To handle and keep classified documents and correspondence.
questions.explanation: A secret registry exists to receive, store, control, and process classified documents and correspondence securely.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What does Schemes of Service refer to in the public service?
options: A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
questions.explanation: Schemes of Service is the reference document that states the duties of each post, the method of entry, qualification requirements, and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: How does the attendance register distinguish between staff who arrive on time and those who are late?
questions.explanation: The attendance register is marked with a red line each morning so that late arrivals sign below it and punctual staff sign above it.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should a minute contain in addition to a summary of the facts of the case?
options: A detailed financial analysis in every case.
A list of all staff involved.
questions.explanation: A good minute should contain a summary of the facts together with the officer's recommendations and conclusions so that action can be considered on a clear record.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which quality best describes a good minute?
options: It should be written in a conversational tone.
It should be verbose and include all personal thoughts.
It should be written only by senior officers.
It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.
questions.explanation: A good minute should be brief, factual, well reasoned, and free from grammatical errors, spelling mistakes, and ambiguity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Apart from the officer handing over, which officials should receive copies of the handing-over note?
options: The Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor.
questions.explanation: Copies of the handing-over note should go to the Head of Department, the Personnel Officer, and the officer's successor so that continuity and accountability are maintained.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Into which two classes should official files generally be grouped?
questions.explanation: Official files are generally grouped into subject files and personal files so that correspondence and records can be organized according to their purpose and ownership.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, as well as what?
options: A list of all staff involved.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (The officer's recommendations and conclusions.). A minute should contain a summary of the facts of the case, the officer's recommendations, and his conclusions.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which of the following best defines the term "a key characteristic of a good minute" according to official standards?
options: It should only be written by senior officers.
It should be verbose and include all personal thoughts.
It should be brief, factual, and free from grammatical errors.
It should be written in a conversational tone.
questions.explanation: A good minute should be brief, factual, well reasoned, and free from grammatical errors, spelling mistakes, and ambiguity.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Why is it important for a chairman to prepare adequately for a meeting?
options: To show off his intelligence to the members.
To give the Secretariat less work to do.
To waste time before the meeting starts.
To ensure a smooth and effective control of the meeting and earn the respect of the members.
questions.explanation: Correct option: D (To secure a smooth and effective control of the meeting and earn the respect of the members..). It is only by preparing adequately that the chairman can assure himself of an effective control of the meeting and earn the full respect of the members.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option correctly addresses: A secretary should send out the notice of meeting and agenda to all members at least how many days before the meeting?
questions.explanation: Meeting notices should be sent early enough for members to prepare, so the agenda is likely to be at least the required notice period.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: What should characterize a good minute of a meeting?
options: It should record jokes and informal remarks.
It should be as verbose as possible.
It should mainly reflect the secretary's personal opinions.
It should be a faithful and accurate summary of what transpired.
questions.explanation: A good minute should provide a faithful and accurate summary of what transpired at the meeting so that the record can be relied upon afterward.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which office should sign a handing-over note?
options: Both the officer handing over and the officer taking over.
questions.explanation: The secretary signs the handing-over note because that office records and certifies the transfer of responsibility.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars

questions.question: Which option correctly addresses: What should a Schedule Officer have, regarding their writing?
questions.explanation: A schedule officer should have neat, legible writing because official schedules must be clear and easily understood.
questions.sourceDocument: Federal Civil Service Handbook and Circulars
